2606.17791.title: The Slop Paradox: How Synthetic Standardization Erodes Clinical Uncertainty and Cross-Modal Alignment in AI-Rewritten Radiology Reports
2606.17791.paper_summary: AI-assisted clinical documentation tools increasingly summarize, standardize, and reformat radiology reports using large language models (LLMs). We present a controlled measurement of the resulting information degradation. Using 450 chest X-ray reports from the Indiana University dataset, we generate synthetic versions via three realistic LLM rewriting tasks: EHR summarization, standardized rewriting, and teaching case preparation. We measure entity erosion (via medical NER), hedging collapse (loss of clinical uncertainty language), and cross-modal alignment degradation (via BiomedCLIP image-text similarity). Our central finding is a dissociation between information loss and cross-modal fidelity. EHR summarization is the most destructive at the content level, eroding 51.4% of clinical entities and 43.7% of hedging language, yet it preserves image-text alignment almost entirely (a 2.5% drop). The two tasks meant to produce cleaner training data, standardized rewriting and teaching case preparation, do the reverse: they preserve more entities (26.8% and 29.3% eroded) but cause 14.9-16.5% alignment drops, six to seven times those of EHR summarization. We term this the slop paradox: rewriting that makes clinical text look cleaner for multimodal training is precisely what pulls it away from the image. Contrary to our pre-specified hypothesis, rare pathologies were not preferentially degraded: across nine rare-versus-common comparisons, no difference survived multiple-comparison correction, and nominal differences ran in the opposite direction (common > rare), so contamination is invisible to condition-specific monitoring. The dominant determinant of degradation is the type of AI rewriting task, not the clinical content. These findings bear on multimodal medical AI dataset construction and the governance of AI-assisted clinical documentation.
2606.17791.paper_summary_zh: AI輔助的臨床文檔工具越來越多地使用大型語言模型（LLMs）來總結、標準化和重新格式化放射學報告。我們呈現了對結果信息劣化的控制測量。利用印第安納大學數據集中的450份胸部X光報告，我們通過三個現實的LLM重寫任務生成合成版本：電子健康記錄（EHR）總結、標準化重寫和教學案例準備。我們測量了實體侵蝕（通過醫療NER）、模糊語言崩潰（臨床不確定性語言的損失）和跨模態對齊劣化（通過BiomedCLIP圖像-文本相似性）。我們的主要發現是信息損失與跨模態忠實度之間的脫節。EHR總結在內容層面上是最具破壞性的，侵蝕了51.4%的臨床實體和43.7%的模糊語言，但幾乎完全保持了圖像-文本對齊（僅下降2.5%）。旨在生成更乾淨訓練數據的兩個任務，標準化重寫和教學案例準備，則正好相反：它們保留了更多的實體（侵蝕率分別為26.8%和29.3%），但導致了14.9-16.5%的對齊下降，是EHR總結的六到七倍。我們將此稱為“混亂悖論”：使臨床文本在多模態訓練中看起來更乾淨的重寫，恰恰是將其拉離圖像的原因。與我們預先指定的假設相反，罕見病理並未受到優先劣化：在九項罕見與常見的比較中，沒有差異在多重比較修正後存活，名義上的差異則朝相反方向（常見 > 罕見）發展，因此污染對於特定條件的監測是不可見的。劣化的主要決定因素是AI重寫任務的類型，而不是臨床內容。這些發現對於多模態醫療AI數據集的構建和AI輔助臨床文檔的治理具有重要意義。
2606.17791.author: Samar Ansari et.al.
